chunks.title: I have not received invoice for e-signing
chunks.text:
Valmo captain payments are done every Friday.

Example: Payment on 13th February 2026

This payment will include:

- Delivered shipments between 26th January to 8th February, but only if the shipment was Inscanned in the week 26th January to 1st February (or any older week).

- RVP shipments picked up in the week 26th January to 1st February.

You can view the status of your latest payment cycle in the Captain Panel (Link: https://partner-app.valmo.in/ (https://partner-app.valmo.in/)) within 'Payments tab' under Payment details column. The key reasons for invoice not generating are as follows

- Hold Negative: Loss > Earnings for the cycle, hence no payment is generated. The loss amount is to be adjusted in the next cycle

- No Payment: Only Loss and no earnings for this cycle. Hence no payment is generated. The loss amount is to be adjusted in the next cycle

- GST Defaulter Hold: GST defaulter is an entity that has not fulfilled mandatory GST filing or payment requirements within the prescribed timelines. As per statutory requirements, payments cannot be released until the GST issue is resolved. We request you to please complete the necessary GST filing or payment at the earliest. Once the compliance is completed, the eligible payment will be processed in the subsequent payment cycle.

- Risk is above threshold (COD pendency): amount of Cash on Delivery (COD) money pending or not yet settled for an account has become higher than the allowed limit. As per policy guidelines, payments cannot be released until the pending COD issues are resolved. We request you to please clear the pending COD is completed at the earliest. Once the risk level is within the defined threshold, the eligible payment will be processed in the subsequent payment cycle.

- Risk is above threshold (COD + Shipment pendency ): Combined risk from pending Cash on Delivery (COD) payments and undelivered shipments has exceeded the allowed limit for the account. As per policy guidelines, payments cannot be released until the pending COD and shipment issues are resolved. We request you to please clear the pending COD and ensure shipments are completed at the earliest. Once the risk level is within the defined threshold, the eligible payment will be processed in the subsequent payment cycle.

- Payment Hold due to GST>20 lakh: Payment is hold due to the GST is above 20 Lakhs. As per statutory requirements, invoicing and payout cannot be processed without a valid GST number. We request you to please provide your GST number at the earliest. Once the GST number is updated and verified, eligible payouts and invoicing will be processed in the subsequent cycle. If you are facing any issues while updating your GST details, please raise a support ticket so that we can assist you.

- Hold by ops: AMs/Ops can update the payment status on O-Paper, which may result in payment processing being put on hold for a partner. Billing details are prepared weekly on Mondays, invoices are generated on Wednesdays, and payment processing is carried out on Fridays. However, even if an invoice has been generated and signed by the captain, AMs/PEMs/Ops may choose to hold the payment due to any identified fraud or discrepancies.

- Onboarding Issue: You are not registered on O-paper. Registration on O-Paper is MANDATORY for us to process payments. Please use the link (https://enprodopmeesho.opaper.in/admin/dashboard (https://enprodopmeesho.opaper.in/admin/dashboard)) to complete your registration.

- F&F Vendor: As per our records, the invoice has not been generated because you have decided to discontinue with Valmo. We are sorry to see you go. As per our SOP, all payments effective from the opt-out date will be adjusted and released as part of the Full and Final settlement, which will be processed 45 days from the date of opt-out. We request you to wait until then to receive your payment. If you reconsider and wish to continue working with us, please reach out to your AM/PEMs—we would be happy to have you back.

- Grocery defaulter: The invoice has not been generated because your payout has been placed on hold due to grocery vertical payout restrictions, as there are pending grocery orders or COD amounts associated with your account. As per policy, payments cannot be released until these pending amounts are resolved. We request you to please clear the pending COD and complete any open grocery orders at the earliest. Once the pending issues are resolved, the eligible payment will be processed in the subsequent payment cycle.

- Negative balance: Loss > Earnings for the cycle, hence no payment is generated. The loss amount is to be adjusted in the next cycle

For further queries, raise a ticket using the 'Raise Ticket' button below and we will assist you at the earliest.

You can view the status of your latest payment cycle in the Captain Panel (Link: https://partner-app.valmo.in/ (https://partner-app.valmo.in/)) within 'Payments tab'.

For further queries, raise a ticket using the 'Raise Ticket' button below and we will assist you at the earliest

chunks.text: I am unable to locate my current or past invoices in the system, which is affecting tracking and reconciliation. The invoices are not visible under the billing or transaction history section, possibly due to access restrictions, system errors, or delayed uploads. This needs to be checked to ensure proper financial documentation and record maintenance.

chunks.title: I am unable to close BTS for my pilot
chunks.text: I am unable to close the BTS for my pilot, as the system is not allowing the status to be updated or the closure action to be completed. This may be due to pending mandatory fields, approval dependencies, or backend validation issues. The delay in closure is impacting tracking and reporting, and requires immediate technical or process-level support.

chunks.title: I want to update my pilot account details
chunks.text: I would like to update my pilot account details, as the current information is either outdated or incorrect. This update is required to ensure smooth operations, accurate communication, and proper financial processing. Kindly assist with the process or enable the necessary changes at the earliest.

chunks.title: My pilot does not have onboarding documents
chunks.text: My pilot does not have the required onboarding documents available in the system, which is preventing smooth operational processing and compliance verification. The documents may not have been uploaded, approved, or mapped correctly during onboarding. This needs to be reviewed and updated to avoid further delays or operational impact.

chunks.title: Valmo SLA & Hardstop timelines — when shipments breach, become loss-eligible, and are marked lost
chunks.text:
Debit value for any loss = Shipment Value + Logistics Cost. Timelines (D-day counted from in-scan / RTO-mark / shortage-mark):
- FM Forward & FM RTO, FMSC & LMSC Forward & RTO: must connect to next hub within 48 hrs of in-scan. Breach on D3, loss-eligible (hardstop) on D5, marked LOST on D6.
- LM Forward: deliver or RTO within 5 days of in-scan. Breach on D5, loss-eligible D7, marked LOST D8.
- LM RTO: connect to LMSC within 48 hrs of RTO marking. Breach D3, loss-eligible D5, marked LOST D6.
- Shortage (bag/shipment): in-scan at any location within 96 hrs of shortage-marked date. Breach D7, loss-eligible D10, marked LOST D11 (per Shortage SOP 3.0).
- In-transit: breach D7, loss-eligible D10, marked LOST D11.
Liability: if the complete trip is not received at destination, the ORIGIN facility is debited. If only partial shipments are pending at destination, the DESTINATION facility is debited.
tags: when does shipment get marked lost
D3 D5 D6 D7 D8 loss
kitne din me loss lagta hai

chunks.title: Bag/Shipment shortage must be marked within 6 hours of vehicle arrival
chunks.text: The Destination Facility must mark any Bag Shortage or Shipment Shortage in the system within SIX (6) HOURS of the vehicle arrival timestamp. This applies to both National Line Haul (NLH) and Regional Line Haul (RLH). If the shortage is not recorded within 6 hours, default liability falls on the Destination Facility — without any recourse to CCTV or other evidence.
tags: how many hours to mark shortage

chunks.title: Shortage liability framework & CCTV evidence rules
chunks.text: If a shortage stays unresolved beyond 5 days of being marked, the company notifies both Origin and Destination facilities; both must submit valid CCTV within 72 hours. Default liability when evidence is missing: (1) if BOTH origin and destination fail to provide evidence → ORIGIN facility; (2) if destination fails but origin provides valid proof → DESTINATION; (3) if a cross-dock facility fails and that causes proven attribution → CROSS-DOCK facility. Valid CCTV requirements: In-transit footage = continuous loading→sealing→departure, single dock camera, max 2 hours. Bag-shortage footage = seal cutting→reapplication, single dock cam, max 2 hours. Shipment-shortage footage = bagging/debagging with seal-tag application clearly visible, max 15 minutes, one camera per bagging station.
tags: cctv kitne ghante me dena hai

chunks.title: RLH Trip Sheet — signing, pre-alert annexure, and 60-day filing
chunks.text: For Regional Line Haul (RLH, multi-touchpoint vehicles), the Origin Facility prepares a Trip Sheet stating bags loaded per destination. It must be signed by the Driver and Origin Supervisor, stamped & signed by the Sort Center Security Desk, and a scanned copy annexed to the Pre-Alert email at departure. At unloading, driver + destination staff jointly count bags; any shortage is recorded on the Trip Sheet contemporaneously and signed by the driver, and updated in the system within 6 hours. The Sort Center must file and preserve the original Trip Sheet for a minimum of 60 days. If the SC fails to produce the Trip Sheet on request → liability on Origin; if driver misplaced it (noted in pre-alert) → Transport Vendor; if trip sheet confirms shortage at one stop and others confirm correct receipt → Transport Vendor.

chunks.title: Facility Pendency reconciliation cycle (7-day, Monday report → Friday loss-mark)
chunks.text: Facility Pendency = shipments inbound-scanned at a facility (vehicle arrival / in-scan / excess / misroute) but not yet out-scanned. Fixed 7-calendar-day reconciliation cycle. Every Monday the company shares a Consolidated Facility Pendency Report listing shipments pending >3 days with ageing from first scan. The vendor must clear all reported pendency by Thursday of the same week; anything unresolved is irrevocably Loss-Marked on Friday. Issues impacting clearance must be raised in writing by Wednesday; only items genuinely beyond the vendor's control roll over (manpower shortage / high volume do NOT count). Once Loss-Marked, no attribution changes/disputes; vendor submits a Credit Note for the total value by the last calendar day of the month.

chunks.title: In-Transit Pendency — 5-day flag, 72-hour evidence, external factors
chunks.text: A trip/vehicle departed from origin but not received at destination within 5 days is flagged as In-Transit Pendency. The origin vendor is notified and must furnish within 72 hours: Pre-Alert email copy, Delivery Challan signed/stamped by SC outbound desk security, and valid CCTV. The destination must revert on the pre-alert email within 5 days of departure confirming received/not. If origin provides valid departure proof and destination did not revert → liability on Destination. If proofs are invalid/incomplete, origin gets +24 hours, else shipments are deemed lost and debited. External factors (police custody, sales-tax detention, accident) reported on the pre-alert thread with supporting docs may get up to 10 extra days; but detention due to improper documentation by origin → origin liable.

chunks.title: Lost-shipment callout — raise ticket within 7 days; where to check losses
chunks.text: For any callout on Lost Shipments, raise a ticket within 7 days of loss marking on the VALMO_Support link (Kapture self-serve portal). Loss/lost shipment details are visible in the Captain Panel → Loss Management Tab (AWB-wise: days in system, point of loss, risk amount, days left to prevent). The Loss Management cards: 'Loss to be Marked' needs immediate action; Extreme Risk = act within 1 day; High Risk = act within ~3 days. Loss-related issues are raised on Kapture, not WhatsApp/Gmail/Excel.
tags: where to see losses captain panel

chunks.title: DRS closure (LM & FM) — must close before 12 AM, COD + OTP steps
chunks.text: All DRS must be closed daily before 12 AM. Path: Log10 → RTO → DRS Closure → search FE name. Scan undelivered shipments (if physically present scan the waybill; if missing click Mark Lost; if damaged scan + select 'Mark as Tampered' reason). For RVP returns choose 'Scan RVP Shipments', scan packet, print new RVP label. The FE can only be validated after ALL waybills (undelivered + RVP) are scanned. Then enter COD amount collected (must be ≤ total COD), click Verify OTP, enter the pilot's OTP, and Close DRS. You cannot open a new DRS for an FE until the existing one is closed. Lost shipment or short COD is deducted from the FE's payout; the FE has 7 days to bring back a 'Lost in Field' shipment and scan it (then paid in next cycle).

chunks.title: Creating a DRS (runsheet) for an FE in Last Mile
chunks.text: To create a runsheet (DRS) for a Field Executive: Log10 → Inventory → Create DRS → select the FE from the Employee dropdown → click Create → scan the shipments to assign (scanned list shows on the side) → click Done. The runsheet is then assigned to that FE. The DRS lifecycle: Pending (FE not yet accepted) → In Transit (FE accepted; only one active trip allowed) → Completed (FE updated every shipment) → Closed (FE returned and DC closed the DRS).

chunks.title: Receiving an inbound trip — mark arrived, unload, scan manifests, validate, complete
chunks.text: To receive an inbound trip: Trips → Inbound Trips → select trip → Mark Arrived → Confirm (status turns green/Completed-arrived). Click Unload → a Closure screen appears → click Pending Manifests to view pending bags → scan all manifests (bags) in the scan box (each shows success; check Pending vs Scanned count) → click Validate ('Validated Successfully') → click Complete Trip → Confirm. Alternative path: Inventory → Closure → select Trip → enter Trip ID → Unload. If any bag is short, check Pending Manifests, update reason (Shortage/Duplicate) and send a shortage proof email. To send RTO back, a Completed trip is converted using 'Convert to Outbound' (IB→OB).

chunks.title: Connection sheet, outbound trip creation and dispatch (OTP / vehicle image)
chunks.text: Connection: Trips → Connection Sheets → Create Connection → select destination → Create Connection Sheet → scan manifest IDs → Update Connection (status becomes LINKED). Outbound trip: Outbound Trips → Create Trip → fill vendor (e.g. Loadshare), vehicle number, driver name/contact, route → Add Load → scan the Connection ID → Update Trip. Use Print to download challan/shipment list — once printed the trip cannot be edited. Then Dispatch → enter the driver's OTP (sent via WhatsApp/SMS) OR, if OTP not received, upload an image of the vehicle showing the load with the driver → Confirm. Trip stages: Pending (no challans), Loaded (challans added, not dispatched), Dispatched, Completed.

chunks.title: RTO bagging and Misroute bagging (wrong facility) process
chunks.text: RTO bagging: RTO → RTO Manifest → Create Manifest → scan a new bag seal ID into Manifest ID → select destination → scan RTO shipments → Lock Manifest → confirm Yes. Misroute (wrong-facility) bagging: Inventory → Exceptions → select 'Scan as Wrong Facility Shipment' → scan the misrouted shipments (Force Inscan brings them into local inventory) → then create a manifest for the correct destination and lock it. Misroute marking can also use 'Migrate AWB' → select the migrate location.

chunks.title: Pilot (FE) onboarding in the Valmo app — Aadhaar/DigiLocker/PAN/bank, then DC adds in Captain Panel
chunks.text: FE/pilot onboarding: the mobile number must be unique, not linked to any existing account, and Aadhaar-linked. In the Valmo app (Google Play): register mobile → Verify OTP → Update Profile → Continue with DigiLocker → enter Aadhaar number → Aadhaar OTP → 6-digit DigiLocker PIN → allow consent → system fetches details → submit Aadhaar details (name must EXACTLY match PAN) → Verify PAN → enter vehicle details → enter bank details (account holder, account number, IFSC) → Submit; all steps show 'Completed' → select preferred pincode and DC. Then the DC owner fills the pilot's rate card and adds the pilot. Finally the DC Captain completes onboarding in the Captain Panel (https://partner-app-int.valmo.in/) → Pilot Management Tab → Add New Pilot → enter & verify mobile (name auto-reflects) → Fill Rate Card (mandatory) → complete pilot details → Submit. Pilot becomes active.

chunks.title: Pilot onboarding errors — 'already exists', active at another hub, blocked rider
chunks.text: 'Pilot already exists on your hub' = the pilot is already onboarded. If a pilot is active at another hub, they must close the running BTS and change DC in the app. If onboarding fails, re-onboard the pilot (recheck mobile number, mandatory fields, rate card, documents, then retry). If a rider is blocked / FE not working: contact your AM, check for pending courses under 'Escalation to Excellence', ask the AM to check 'Fraud Termination Tracker 2.0' for the blocking reason; if no info, raise a ticket with Valmo. To change HUB / update bank / request DC change, use the Work Details section in the Captain Panel; DC change applies after approval.
tags: pilot already exists on your hub

chunks.title: Captain Panel CPS (Cost Per Shipment) — green/red, rate cards, Ops Cluster Head approval
chunks.text: CPS (Cost Per Shipment) monitors delivery cost vs Target CPS (Goal). If current cost < target → GREEN/Good. If current cost > target → attention/RED, and shipment volume can be cut while nearby hubs with lower CPS get more load. To recover volume, reduce FE rate cards: CPS Tab → Edit Pilot Rates → enter new rates (must not exceed goal cost) → enter COD Base Rate (Prepaid/Consolidated auto-calculate) → Submit. If submitted rates are ABOVE the current hub cost, it goes to an approval process — contact the Ops Cluster Head (status 'Approval Pending'; FE can keep working meanwhile). If ≤ hub cost, auto/approved (green). Updated cost reflects in the Captain Panel at 2 AM daily. Changing rate cards for inactive pilots (0% / 0 avg delivery) does NOT impact CPS.
tags: rate card approval ops cluster head

chunks.title: DC Capacity management — recommended capacity, RVP minimum 20%, sale-day 1.5x, 2 PM cutoff
chunks.text: Recommended DC capacity is calculated from number of active pilots × pilot productivity. The Hub Incharge/Captain enters Forward (FWD) and Reverse Pickup (RVP) capacity in the Captain Panel and saves it; it's used for the NEXT day's manifestation planning, so finalize changes before 2 PM. You may enter higher or lower than recommended (a warning like 'Inputs very high – need extra pilots' appears but Forward capacity still saves). Reverse Pickup capacity must meet a minimum of 20% of forward capacity — below that the system errors 'Input is very Low'. During sale events and Mondays, the capacity consumed is 1.5× what the DC provides (e.g. 800 provided → up to 1200 consumed).

chunks.title: Captain Panel Cash Pendency — D1–D4 risk buckets and submission methods
chunks.text: The Cash Pendency module tracks pending COD cash by age: D1 = Low Risk (1 day), D2 = High Risk (2 days), D3 = Extreme Risk (3 days, near breach), D4 = Breached (4+ days) → at D4 the COD deposit timeline is breached and the DC load becomes ZERO (immediate action needed). Submission methods: (1) UPI — online, fast, recommended; (2) NEFT/Net Banking — online, may take processing time; (3) CMS Partner — but CMS personnel may not be available daily; (4) Manual Bank Deposit into the Meesho account — slowest, only if others unavailable.

chunks.title: Captain Panel Payment module — earnings breakdown, invoice/GST gate
chunks.text: The Payment module shows weekly/monthly payouts with a summary: Total Base Pay (forward + reverse deliveries, kirana expenses, self-pickup), Total Incentives (conversion + ad-hoc), Total Adjustments & Taxes (GST CGST/SGST/IGST, RVP consumables, TDS), Total Losses (value + count + credit note number), and Total Earnings, plus the payment-credited date. IMPORTANT: if invoices are not accepted or GST not filed for previous cycles, payments cannot be processed — the captain must accept the pending invoice or file GST and share confirmation before payments are credited. A payment cycle runs Monday to Sunday. Invoice/Credit Note/Debit Note can be downloaded from the Payments tab.

chunks.title: Captain Panel Service Area — My Service Area, Find Shipment, Misroute
chunks.text: The Service Area module has three sections: (1) My Service Area — current and previously marked serviceable areas (drawn via Atlas), serviced pincodes, area-update history, expected Meesho & Valmo orders. (2) Find Shipment Location — scan AWB to see shipping address, exact location and how many past deliveries were done there. (3) Misroute Shipments — shipments incorrectly mapped to a DC; filter by in-scan date range to find items physically in your area but marked for another DC. Note: if a shipment's address is outside the CURRENT service area but was in a PREVIOUSLY selected area, it still arrives at the DC and must be delivered.

chunks.title: PBCA — Performance Based Capacity Action (formula, 70% cut, 10-day recovery)
chunks.text: PBCA = Performance Based Capacity Action, applied when a hub underperforms versus other 3P hubs in the same pincode (evaluated at pincode level, then hub level). Performance = Conversion (FAD + RAD) − ZRTO, where FAD = Forward Attempted Deliveries, RAD = Reverse Attempted Deliveries, ZRTO = Zero Return to Origin. Delta = best 3P/BIC hub performance − Valmo hub performance. If the Valmo hub is lower (positive delta), up to 70% of its load/capacity is reduced. The hub then has 10 days to improve. If it performs better than the best 3P hub (Best in Class), capacity recovery applies — up to 80% load increase — but only after 10 days from when PBCA was imposed. The highest-performing 3P hub is always the benchmark.

chunks.title: Atlas tool — drawing hub serviceability (Locus map, hexagons, overlapping areas)
chunks.text: Atlas is a Locus-map tool to draw/mark a hub's serviceable area using map hexagons. Login with your registered email; only the vendor owner can use it for their hub. Steps: Draw Serviceability → select hub/DC code → check location pin → Start Updating → select hexagons (click/drag) → use 'Switch to Removing' to fix mistakes, 'Adding' to add more → Stop Updating → Confirm Changes. View demand via Metric Visualization → select pincode → Get Heatmap → pick a metric (order count is the daily average over the last 7 days). There are NO exclusive areas — multiple DCs can draw over the same area; overlapping orders are split, with better-performing DCs getting higher allocation. (Note: the hexagon size is stated as 500 m in the step-by-step but 200 m in the FAQ — worth confirming with ops.)

chunks.title: TMS — vehicle open/locked states and IB→OB conversion for RTO
chunks.text: In TMS: a vehicle stays in OPEN state while challans are created and the trip is built (one vehicle = one route = one Trip ID; only onboarded vendors and open vehicles appear in dropdowns). Once dispatched with the driver OTP, the vehicle goes to LOCKED state and the trip can't be amended. At each destination, arrival is marked (GPS/Driver app/Opstech), and only then 'Convert inbound trip to outbound' (IB→OB) is used to send RTO back to origin. The vehicle returns to OPEN (available for next dispatch) only after arrival has been scanned at ALL nodes in the route. Once arrival is marked in Opstech, the vehicle/driver is released immediately for reuse. Manual coloading vehicle-number format: COL_<Source SC Code>_Date (use a random phone number and image upload instead of OTP).
tags: one vehicle one route one trip

chunks.title: TMS error resolutions — transporter/vehicle/driver not in dropdown, mapping, convert-to-outbound
chunks.text: Common TMS errors & L1 fixes: 'Can't see Transporter in list' / 'Vehicle or Driver not in dropdown' → transporter/vehicle/driver not onboarded → contact Spriha/Moin/Revant to onboard in TMS. 'Error while submitting challan' → transporter not mapped to the SC → contact Spriha/Moin/Revant to map it. 'Either driver or vehicle not available' → identify the node that hasn't marked arrival and ask them to mark it. 'TMS error: driver and vehicle not available for trip' → the vehicle/driver is part of another active TMS trip (some node hasn't marked arrival) → ask that driver to mark entry on the pending node (install Driver App and mark arrival). 'Node XYZ not in Hub Mapping' → wrong Line-haul type chosen → pick correctly: SC-SC = NLH, SC-LMDC = RLH, SC-Gateway = NLH, SC-PC/SC-FMHub = FM Carting. 'Trip is closed' → create a new trip / send next day. 'Trip not eligible for convert to outbound (400)' → truck already arrived at next node, or route has >50 nodes → send next day or add challans in batches (25 at a time). If a vendor is not mapped to the site, challans will not be dispatched.

chunks.title: RVP doorstep QC — the 5 questions, same-shade = same colour, ICUD, packet missing → Lost
chunks.text: The new app-driven RVP doorstep QC asks up to 5 questions, decided per product: (1) Is the product category matching? (2) Is the design the same? — pick 'Same Design' even for slight changes, 'Different' only if completely different. (3) Is the colour matching? — different SHADES of the same primary colour count as SAME colour (e.g. maroon vs bright red = Same). (4) Is the quantity the same? (5) Is the product damaged (broken/torn/stained/worn)? Flow: Start trip → Verify OTP (ask customer) → take product → upload clear images (front/back/side; no junk images) → answer QC → Finish → the app decides 'Pickup item' or 'Do not Pickup item'. ICUD (Image Capture at User Doorstep) = images captured at original delivery, used as the reference for category/design/colour. Variant A = 3 questions (category, design, colour); Variant B = 1 question (does it match the ICUD image). If an RVP packet is missing and can't be scanned → Mark as Lost.

chunks.title: Secondary QC at the DC (hub) — 3 images, approve/reject, failed → not connected to LMSC
chunks.text: Secondary QC must be done at the DC for ALL return shipments to control fraud and reduce losses. Process: Scan AWB → scan the QR/packet ID → the QC window opens, capture 3 images (Side, Back, Front) → verify the FE's responses for category/design → click Approve if matched, or Reject + Next if mismatched → Finish. On success, take a print label. On QC Fail, the system shows 'QC Failed'. Note: Secondary-QC-failed shipments are prevented from connecting to the LMSC; for now no debit is applied to hubs for those shipments.

chunks.title: FE Recommendation system — assign reverse pickups to the FE who delivered earlier
chunks.text: The FE Recommendation system (Loadshare/Log10) recommends the Field Executive who previously delivered the same shipment, to speed up Reverse Pickup (RVP) assignment. Path: Pickup → Manage Reverse Pickup → Apply Filters → find the 'Recommended FE' column (highlighted blue) → Actions → Assign FE → Confirm ('FE assigned Successfully', status becomes Assigned). Use the 'Search Recommended' filter to find all pickups for a given FE, and '+ Assign FE' for bulk assignment. To assign someone other than the recommendation, pick from the dropdown. To change an assigned pickup, use '+ Reassign FE'. A runsheet can only be reassigned until the FE accepts it on the app.

chunks.title: Suspicious Attempt Quality Panel — verifying fake delivery attempts (FE Audit)
chunks.text: A shipment is a 'Suspicious Attempt' when the pilot neither called the customer nor visited the location. The captain audits these via Log10 → POD → FE Audit tab. Step 1: call the customer and confirm whether they still want the order and whether the pilot attempted. Step 2: if the customer does NOT want it → Mark as Done; if they WANT it → Mark as Priority. Priority handling: if the rider is still out, call the pilot to re-attempt; if the shipment already returned to hub, it appears in the next-day DRS with an AWB reminder when creating the runsheet — assign it again. If the customer doesn't answer, retry after ~30 min and follow the 3-attempt calling guideline; keep trying unless they explicitly cancel. Goal: reduce suspicious attempts and RTO%.

chunks.title: First Attempt Pickup (FAP) vs Reattempt Pickup (RAP) and how to improve FAP
chunks.text: First Attempt Pickup (FAP) = shipment collected on the first pickup visit. Reattempt Pickup (RAP) = collected on a later attempt (customer unavailable / product not ready first time). FAP% = shipments picked on first attempt ÷ shipments sent out for first pickup attempt. RAP% = reattempt pickups completed ÷ shipments sent out for pickup. To improve FAP: start early, plan/strategize the pickup route by distance & timing, call the customer before arrival to confirm readiness, and offer flexible same-day pickup times if they're unavailable. Operational rule '11 to 11': 100% of pickups created the same day must be marked OFP before 11 AM and pendency till 11 AM marked OFD.

chunks.title: LocatR — video-proof recording for unloading/loading (dock QR, desktop only)
chunks.text: LocatR is a cloud video-proof system that links OpsTech scan events to tamper-proof, time-stamped dock video for loss attribution. Dock cameras have unique QR codes (from the LocatR team) that must be clearly visible; the camera must capture the vehicle door and full loading/unloading area; all bag scanning, vehicle sealing and seal-breaking must happen under the same dock camera. No process starts without selecting a dock camera. Unloading: Trips → Inbound → Mark Arrived → Unload → scan the Dock QR (green 'dock verified') → Start Recording → scan manifests under the camera → Complete Trip; then choose 'No Loading/Load Later' (confirm vehicle sealed, recording stops) or 'Continue Loading'. If the dock QR is invalid, the system lists active docks — move the vehicle to one. Recording can be skipped only by selecting a reason (it warns it's proof for shortage/loss). On API failure, Retry or skip with a reason. IMPORTANT: LocatR is NOT supported on handheld devices — use a desktop.

chunks.title: Kapture Self-Serve portal — raising/tracking tickets, payment cycle, feedback & reopen windows
chunks.text: Kapture self-serve portal (https://selfserveapp.kapturecrm.com/support-portals/valmo/login.php): log in with registered email → enter OTP → homepage shows common issues + search. Search/select the issue and review the suggested solution; if unresolved, Raise a Ticket → provide Hub Code, issue description, download & upload the template, and for payment issues enter the dates not received (a payment cycle is Monday–Sunday) and your Enbolt ID → Submit. 'See all tickets' shows date, ticket ID, status and actions; you can Reply (with attachments). A completed ticket can be reopened and feedback (rating) given only within 7 days of closure. Track under My Tickets → Pending / Completed tabs.

chunks.title: Key Valmo tools & support links (Log10/Kapture/Captain Panel/Jarvis/Adhyayan)
chunks.text: Key links/contacts: Log10/Loadshare tech issues → email tech-support@valmo.in. Valmo process issues → Kapture self-serve portal (selfserveapp.kapturecrm.com/support-portals/valmo). Captain Panel (Loss Management, Cash Pendency, Captain Payouts, Service Area, Pilot Rate Card) → https://partner-app.valmo.in/ (or partner-app-int.valmo.in). Loadshare/Log10 web → log10-atlas.loadshare.net. Jarvis (AI assistant for process/SOP doubts) → ai-assistant-trainer Cloud Run URL; mainly for DC Partners (ask Cluster Trainer for access). Adhyayan (full SOPs/PPTs) → sites.google.com/meesho.com/adhyayan/home — AMs only, Meesho email required. Order consumables (bags/seals/bolt seals/RVP flyers) → sites.google.com/meesho.com/valmo-sop/order-consumables. To block a fraud customer: share supporting docs with your AM, who mails the Trust & Safety team. Seller contact number is only in the Loadshare FE app (assign the shipment to the FE, open on FE's mobile, click the seller). Download LM pendency: Log10 → Monitoring Reports → LM Hub Visibility → Forward → LM Forward Pendency Data Dump (filter Inward); RVP pendency: Pickup → Manage Reverse Pickups → Export.

chunks.title: Hub Audit and Lost-in-Field 7-day recovery
chunks.text: Hub Audit: Inventory → Audit → Start Audit → scan all AWBs → Finish Audit → view summary. During a hub audit, other ERP features are inaccessible to users until the audit is finished. Lost in Field: a pilot can bring back a 'Lost in Field' shipment to the hub to be scanned within 7 days of recovery; if found and scanned within 7 days the FE is paid in the next payout cycle.

chunks.title: Damaged/Tampered shipment handling and debit avoidance
chunks.text: A Damaged/Tampered shipment is one received non-deliverable due to liquid damage, unreadable/unscannable barcode-AWB-QR, or a missing AWB label with the number hand-written. Within 6 hours of vehicle arrival, mark it 'Damaged'/'Tampered' in the system and reply to the original Pre-Alert email attaching valid CCTV (per Clause 6, capturing the unloading/discovery) and good-quality images of the packet. Minor cosmetic defects (slight scratches, minor tears) that don't affect contents or AWB scannability are NOT 'damaged/tampered' — they must be processed and forwarded. If proofs are valid and the SOP followed, the vendor is not debited; otherwise the shipment is irreversibly debited to the receiving facility.

chunks.title: Wrong RVP / Dual Scan Mismatch / Secondary QC Fail debits
chunks.text: Wrong RVP picked: if the Meesho Central QC team determines a QC failure, the shipment value is debited to the LM Pilot/Captain. Dual Scan Mismatch and Secondary QC Fail (marked at FM locations during the RTO journey) are eligible for debit, determined by Pareto analysis — if a shipment passed through your node at any point in its journey it may incur a debit.

chunks.title: Common LM app errors — packet mismatch, open DRS, RTO status, OFD/inbound scan
chunks.text: Frequent LM app errors & fixes: 'Packet Mismatch — wrong packet scanned 5 times' → Mark Undel, then add the shipment to another DRS to re-attempt (DEL works only if you scan/type 3 digits of the correct packet ID). 'Field executive has open DRS, close to proceed' → the Ops must close the existing BTS/DRS (and PRS for RVP) before assigning a new one. 'Waybill is in INWARD status, mark it as RTO' → it's not yet in RTO; ask the AM to mark it RTO, then RTO → create manifest → scan seal → select SC → scan AWB. 'Consignment is not eligible for OFD' → force-scan all shipments in INBOUND so they can be dispatched. 'Please unload the manifest before in-scanning the waybill' → unload that trip's manifest first (Inbound Trips → Post Marking Arrived → Unload → scan manifests → Validate → Complete). 'Create POD for OFD shipments and in-scan undelivered/RVP to close DRS/PRS' → mark all OFD shipments DEL/UNDEL first, then close the DRS. To cancel a shipment without OTP → use the 'Customer Refused to Accept' option. DRS delivered shipment keeps reappearing in pending → raise a ticket in Loadshare and inform your AM.
tags: waybill in rto status mark rto

chunks.title: Log10 — create user / add rider (LS ID creation)
chunks.text: To create a Log10 user / LS ID: Log10 → Settings (gear) → User → '+ Create User' → enter Name, DOB, phone, email → select Location (hub/DC) → select User Level (Branch / Field Executive / Admin etc.) → in the Role section Select Role → Add Role (ensure role status is Active) → optionally select Asset and toggle 'Access Export Data' → review → CREATE. Role assignment is mandatory for activation; the user becomes active immediately and appears in the User List. Only users with access to Settings → User can create users.

chunks.title: MM Sort-Centre Kapture tool — shortage evidence submission rules
chunks.text: The Mid-Mile Sort-Centre Kapture tool is used to submit shortage proof when Valmo requests it; a ticket is auto-created per shortage case. Log in (credentials from the Valmo team) → Tickets → 'Assigned to Me' shows tickets with an SLA countdown — evidence must be submitted within SLA. Use the Dispose Ticket tab: Evidence Details + Evidence URL (PUBLIC URL only) + Attachment (MANDATORY, max 100 MB; for large files paste a public URL; a recording link still needs at least one attachment) → Submit. Once submitted, evidence can't be edited and the ticket moves to 'Completed by Me'. Statuses: Replied, Evidence Approved, Evidence Rejected (reason emailed), Closing Post 24Hrs. Shortage cases marked >24 hours after TAT appear only in 'Completed by Me' and need NO evidence. All communication is via the system mail chain.

chunks.title: MM Sort-Centre L.E.A.N. Scorecard — metrics, weightage, locking
chunks.text: The MM Sort-Centre L.E.A.N. Scorecard measures facility performance (Total 100%): Connection Compliance 40% (volume connected within cutoff +30-min buffer ÷ forward dispatched), Pendency Forward 20% (>24h at facility ÷ forward dispatched), Pendency Reverse 20% (>48h ÷ reverse dispatched), Shortage+Hardstop 20% (shortage <24h → previous facility, >24h → current; hardstop = >5 days at facility). Cluster Head score = volume-weighted average of facilities. Zone scores plotted on a bell curve (relative within zone; no cross-zone comparison). Weekly scores lock Sunday 23:59 (Mon 00:00–Sun 23:59), matured score visible by Wednesday. Manpower master data must be updated before the 5th of each month, then frozen. Any AM/CH consistently scoring below 3 must submit an RCA and corrective action within 2 weeks.

chunks.title: NPS & CX I/O — customer experience metrics and fraud/escalation themes
chunks.text: NPS (Net Promoter Score) captures customer sentiment/pain points: no timely updates, delivery delay, FE collecting extra amount, poor FE behaviour, product not delivered. CX I/O (Customer Experience Input & Output) tracks operational escalations: non-OTP deliveries, invalid/missing POD, fake delivery attempts, OTP misuse/fake updates, extra-amount collection, FE misconduct, and FE frauds (product delivered-tracking-updated-RTO with empty box/missing qty/wrong colour/wrong product). Together they help close service gaps, cut escalations and prevent fraud. Key fraud sub-themes to watch: non_otp_delivery, invalid_pod, fake reattempt updates, and product_delivered_tracking_updated_rto_* cases.

chunks.title: Jarvis AI assistant — purpose, login, languages, usage tips
chunks.text: Jarvis is the AI assistant for process & SOP related doubts (mainly for DC Partners; ask your Cluster Trainer to enable access). Login is via User ID and Password. It supports Hindi, English and Marathi. On first use it may take ~10–15 seconds to respond. Ask clear, correctly-spelled, on-topic SOP questions; avoid out-of-topic questions.

chunks.title: First Mile pickup planning, FE assignment and pickup statuses
chunks.text: First Mile pickup planning: Dashboard → Forward → Pickup Planning shows FM pendency by location and aging (bookings before/after 11am, reattempt T-1/T-2/T>2). Fresh pickups should be attempted by 11 AM daily. Track status: Assigned / Picked / Not Picked / Unassigned. Assign FEs three ways: Manual (filter 'Unassigned' → Apply Filters → select → +Assign FE → choose FE → Confirm), Bulk (Upload Assigned Pickup with the sample file), or Direct (search waybills → +Assign FE). Pickup statuses: PCREATED = fresh pendency, PPEND = backdated not picked, PSUCC = not connected to Sort Center. You can reassign an FE only until the runsheet is accepted by the FE on the app. Export gives seller-wise pendency.

chunks.title: First Mile forward bagging (master bag) for the Sort Center
chunks.text: First Mile forward bagging (create a master bag/manifest for the Sort Center daily — 100% completion required): Inventory → Manifest → Create Manifest → scan the bag seal under Manifest ID → select the destination code → the system shows Pending AWB count → scan all waybill numbers (count reduces with each scan) → Lock Manifest → confirm Yes.

chunks.title: IT induction & data security basics (support hours, endpoints, lost device, WPS)
chunks.text: IT Support hours: 9 AM – 9 PM IST, Monday–Sunday. An 'IT Endpoint' includes company laptops, external hard drives, and smartphones/tablets (all of the above). If you lose an IT endpoint, report to the IT team immediately and notify your manager. When stepping away from your desk, lock your computer screen. WPS Office 'don't': do NOT create a WPS ID using your official Meesho email ID.

chunks.title: Captain Panel login and modules overview
chunks.text: Log in to the Captain Panel at https://partner-app.valmo.in/ (or partner-app-int.valmo.in) using your mobile number and OTP (Send OTP). The panel covers: Loss Management (loss-prone shipments, risk amount, days left), Cash Pendency (COD deposit, D1–D4 risk), Payments/Captain Payouts (earnings, invoices, GST), Service Area (serviceable area via Atlas, find shipment, misroute), Pilot Rate Card / CPS (cost per shipment, FE rates), and Pilot Management (onboard/manage/track pilots).

chunks.title: Scans valid + Hardstop SOP followed → reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: log10_scans == valid_connection; hardstop_sop_followed == true. Action: escalate. Loss marked against your shipments will be reversed.

chunks.title: Scans valid + Hardstop SOP NOT followed → no reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: log10_scans == valid_connection; hardstop_sop_followed == false. Action: respond. Reversal not applicable — Hardstop SOP was not followed.

chunks.title: Scans invalid (wrong destination connection) → no reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: log10_scans == wrong_destination_connection. Action: respond. Reversal not applicable — wrong connection destination detected in scans.

chunks.title: Customer Rejection → no forward connection in RTO timeline → no reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: captain_reason == customer_rejection; log10_scans.forward_connection_within_rto_timeline == false. Action: respond. Reversal not applicable — no forward connection within RTO timeline.

chunks.title: Customer Unreachable → shipment not delivered within 7 days → no reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: captain_reason == customer_unreachable; log10_scans.delivered_within_7_days == false. Action: respond. Reversal not applicable — shipment not delivered within 7 days.

chunks.title: Customer denies OTP → <3 attempts or not delivered in 7 days → no reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: captain_reason == customer_denies_otp; log10_scans.delivery_attempts_within_7_days < 3; log10_scans.delivered_within_7_days == false. Action: respond. Reversal not applicable — fewer than 3 delivery attempts within 7 days.

chunks.title: Customer wants shipment post 7 days → <3 attempts or not delivered → no reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: captain_reason == customer_wants_post_7_days; log10_scans.delivery_attempts_within_7_days < 3; log10_scans.delivered_within_7_days == false. Action: respond. Loss not applicable for reversal. Delivery must be attempted 3 times per SOP. Please advise customer to cancel and reorder.

chunks.title: Shipment delivered but couldn't be marked → direct to tech team
chunks.text: Conditions: captain_reason == delivered_but_could_not_mark. Action: respond. Please raise this issue directly with the tech team along with relevant proof of delivery.

chunks.title: Not inscanned to hub + FE amount RECOVERED → reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: captain_reason == shipment_not_inscanned_to_hub; metabase.lm_fe_loss_marked.amount_recovered_from_fe == true. Action: escalate. Loss marked against your shipments will be reversed.

chunks.title: Not inscanned to hub + FE amount NOT recovered + Consignment_Lost status → reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: captain_reason == shipment_not_inscanned_to_hub; metabase.lm_fe_loss_marked.amount_recovered_from_fe == false; log10_scans.last_status == Consignment_Lost. Action: escalate. Loss marked against your shipments will be reversed.

chunks.title: Awaited instruction (seller dependency) + SOP BREACHED → no reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: captain_reason == awaited_instruction_seller_dependency; metabase.seller_dependency_attempts.sop_followed == false. Action: respond. No reversal — Seller Dependency SOP was not followed.

chunks.title: Awaited instruction (seller dependency) + SOP FOLLOWED → reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: captain_reason == awaited_instruction_seller_dependency; metabase.seller_dependency_attempts.sop_followed == true. Action: escalate. Loss marked against your shipments will be reversed.

chunks.title: Wrong shipment/bag received (misrouted, deadline missed) → no reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: captain_reason == wrong_shipment_or_bag_received; log10_scans.misroute_within_2_days == false. Action: respond. No reversal applicable — shipments not routed to correct hub within 2-day misroute deadline.

chunks.title: Correctly misrouted (MISROUTE scan within 2 days) → reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: captain_reason == misrouted_shipment; log10_scans.misroute_scan_within_2_days == true. Action: escalate. Loss will be reversed — misroute was detected within the valid window.

chunks.title: Incorrectly misrouted (back-and-forth beyond limit) → no reversal
chunks.text: Conditions: captain_reason == misrouted_shipment; log10_scans.back_and_forth_misroute_count > 2. Action: respond. No reversal applicable — shipments exceeded maximum misroute allowance.

chunks.title: Hardstop Loss Waiver — captain meets all waiver eligibility conditions
chunks.text: Conditions: captain_tenure_days < 60; prior_hardstop_reversals == 0; invoice_cycles_generated >= 1; current_hardstop_pendency == 0. Action: escalate. Your case meets the eligibility criteria for a hardstop waiver. We are escalating this for review.

chunks.text: Conditions: marked_location_sla_breached == false; assigned_location_sla_breached == false; captain_contesting_attribution == true. Action: escalate. Both partners have submitted evidence. Escalating to the evidence validation team for review.

chunks.text: Conditions: captain_issue == manual_entries_after_scan_failure. Action: respond. For orders with scan failures requiring manual entry, payment is processed in the cycle following your order cycle (per April timeline). Please allow the cycle to complete.

chunks.text: Conditions: ticket_intent == unclear. Action: needs_info. To assist you better, could you please describe the specific issue you're facing, including relevant AWB numbers and what action you are requesting?

chunks.text: Conditions: captain reports scan/system/process error; NO loss is being marked on the captain. Action: respond. We understand you are facing a technical issue. Please contact the Log10 team directly with details of the error (screenshot/error message). They will resolve the technical issue.

chunks.title: Tech Issue + Loss Being Marked on Captain
chunks.text: Conditions: captain reports scan/system/process error OR cannot initialize a process; AND loss IS being marked OR the error is causing loss to be attributed to captain. Action: escalate. We understand there is a technical issue causing incorrect loss marking. Please: 1) Contact the Log10 team with proof (screenshot of the error/system issue). 2) We are also escalating this ticket internally and will resolve the loss once the technical issue is confirmed. You will not be held liable for losses caused by the system error.

chunks.title: Hardstop Loss
chunks.text: Captain received loss AWB details and is contesting the loss marked on them. Apply hardstop rule (7d forward / 5d reverse) and scan validity checks. | Intent: Captain contests a hardstop loss marking | Scenarios: Scans valid + hardstop SOP followed → escalate; Scans valid + hardstop SOP NOT followed → reject; Scans invalid (wrong destination) → reject; Fails to connect after Customer Rejection → reject; Fails to connect after Customer Unreachable → reject; Customer denies cancellation OTP → reject; Customer wants shipment post 7 days → reject; Delivered but couldn't be marked → redirect_tech; Not inscanned to hub + FE amount recovered → escalate; Not inscanned + FE not recovered + Consignment_Lost → escalate; Awaited instruction (seller dep.) — SOP breached → reject; Awaited instruction (seller dep.) — SOP followed → escalate; Wrong shipments received — misroute deadline missed → reject; Shipments correctly misrouted → escalate; Shipments incorrectly misrouted (>2 bounces) → reject | Guardrails: Hardstop waiver allowed only if: captain tenure < 60 days AND zero prior reversals AND at least 1 payment cycle invoiced AND current hardstop pendency = 0.; L2 waive-off approval limit: ₹5,000 per payment cycle.

chunks.title: Shortage Loss
chunks.text: Captain claims shortage loss was wrongly marked. Validate mail evidence to both partners, SLA compliance, and scan data. | Intent: Captain contests a shortage loss attribution | Scenarios: Evidence mails not sent to one or both partners → escalate; Captain unable to mark — SOP followed (tech issue) → redirect_tech; Captain unable to mark — SOP breached → reject; Only one partner shared evidence → reject; Both partners shared evidence — contest needs validation → escalate; Captain claims wrong email ID was shared → reject | Guardrails:

chunks.title: Full and Final Settlement
chunks.text: Check the F&F Dashboard AP Remarks column; respond directly for common statuses, escalate for others. | Intent: Captain wants to close their DC / requests F&F or SD refund | Scenarios: AP Remarks = FnF Done / GST Issue / Signature Pending / Open Pendency → respond; AP Remarks = Deposit Issue / Held by Ops / SD missing → escalate; SD refund — Cost Ops = Refund Done / Pending from Cost Ops → respond; SD refund — Pending from ops / partner dependency → escalate | Guardrails:

chunks.title: Shipment count mismatch in invoice
chunks.text: Compute delta between invoiced and actual counts from the query; respond with the delta template. | Intent: Captain claims FWD + RVP count in invoice doesn't match actual | Scenarios: Compute delta and respond → respond | Guardrails:

chunks.title: Orders & Planning (load / polygon)
chunks.text: Run the Orders and Planning View query; action depends on the L1_Remarks column. | Intent: Captain raises low load, load fluctuation, pin-code change, or RVP reactivation | Scenarios: L1_Remarks = Improve RTO Performance → respond; L1_Remarks = Max volume for polygon → respond; L1_Remarks = Escalate to L2 → escalate; Pin code add/subtract request → escalate; Load made zero without intimation → escalate; RVP load zero / reactivate RVP → escalate; Increase RVP load → escalate | Guardrails:

chunks.text: Four scenarios: how-to, whitelisting, order tracking, quantity mismatch. | Intent: Captain hasn't received consumables or cannot order them | Scenarios: How to place an order → respond; Not whitelisted → respond; Tracking — order Delivered → escalate; Tracking — order Dispatched → respond; Qty mismatch — qty matches + Delivered → escalate; Qty mismatch — qty does NOT match → escalate | Guardrails:

chunks.title: Consumables quality (damage / QC)
chunks.text: Both scenarios route directly to supplier (L3 loop-in). | Intent: Captain reports damaged consumables or secondary QC failure | Scenarios: Damaged consumables → escalate; Secondary QC failure (code mismatch) → escalate | Guardrails:

chunks.text: High-fraud queue. Validate ordered vs claimed packets using the captain payout query before refunding. | Intent: Captain hasn't received payment for RVP consumables | Scenarios: Partial — query count < stated count → escalate; Partial — count matches query → respond; No payment — count ≈ 0 in query → respond; Manual entries post-scan failure → respond | Guardrails:

chunks.text: Two branches: pure tech issue (redirect to Log10) vs tech issue causing a loss (two-track). | Intent: Captain reports a technical / system error | Scenarios: Pure tech issue — no loss marked → respond; Tech issue + loss being marked → escalate | Guardrails:

chunks.title: What is Valmo?
chunks.text: Valmo (Value Mobility) is a third-party logistics player born inside Meesho. It does NOT get special treatment — whichever 3PL is cheapest in a region gets Meesho's volume. Valmo is a network of local third-party logistics partners forming a chain of nodes. Nothing is owned by Valmo — sort centers, gateways, trucks, delivery centers are all contracted to partners.

chunks.title: Supply Chain Flow
chunks.text:
Seller → (milk run pickup) → First Mile (FM) Hub → FM Sorting Center → National Line Haul → Last Mile (LM) Sorting Center → Delivery Center (DC) → Captain → Customer

- **FM Hub**: Picks up parcels from sellers, bags them, sends to FM Sort Center
- **FM Sort Center**: Sorts to LM Sort Centers (primary + secondary sort)
- **National Line Haul**: Truck movement from FM Sort Center to LM Sort Center
- **LM Sort Center**: Sorts packages for DC-level delivery
- **DC (Delivery Center)**: The last-mile node. Captains (delivery partners) operate from here.
- **Captain**: The delivery executive. Delivers forward shipments, picks up RTO/RVP.

chunks.title: Valmo's Role
chunks.text: Valmo primarily operates on **last-mile (LM) stakeholders**. The DC-level operations and captain management is Valmo's core business.

chunks.title: 1. Hardstop Loss
chunks.text: A shipment is at a particular hub for **more than 5 days (120 hours)** without being connected to the next node. The system marks it as hardstop loss. There are 10 sub-scenarios (see hardstop_scenarios.md).

chunks.title: 2. Shortage Loss
chunks.text: Shipment is sent from Node A to Node B. Node B does not receive it. B marks it as shortage. Both nodes are asked for evidence (CCTV footage). Loss is attributed to one node based on evidence. If both seem right → trucker is asked. If trucker also seems right → loss attributed to Meesho.

chunks.title: 3. In-Transit Loss
chunks.text: Shipment or bag is lost in transit from Node A to Node B. Simpler than shortage — no evidence process.

chunks.title: COD (Cash on Delivery)
chunks.text:
- ~75% of Valmo's orders are COD (tier 2/tier 3 cities, cash-based)
- Captain delivers parcel, collects cash from customer
- Captain deposits cash at the hub
- CMS (Cash Management System) vendors (e.g., Airtel, ~5 vendors total) collect cash from hubs and deposit to Meesho's bank account
- **COD Pendency**: If hub has outstanding cash not yet submitted to CMS, pendency builds up. E.g., hub owes ₹10,000 but only ₹9,000 submitted → ₹1,000 pendency.

chunks.title: Kapture CRM
chunks.text:
- Ticket management system (CRM)
- Captains raise tickets via **self-serve portal** (separate website with pre-populated fields) which creates tickets in Kapture via webhook
- L1 agents work tickets from Kapture queue
- Fields: ticket_id, partner_id, queue, sub_queue, hub_code, AWBs, description, attachments

chunks.title: Metabase
chunks.text:
- Internal BI/analytics tool
- Contains queries for: loss attribution, AWB scan history, payment status, invoice status, COD pendency, hub performance, etc.
- L1 agents run queries here to validate captain claims

chunks.title: Log10 (console.valmo.in)
chunks.text:
- Ops tracking system
- Shows real-time shipment scan history
- Login: mobile number + OTP
- Used for: checking if shipment was scanned, connection timeline, RTO status, delivery attempts

chunks.title: Captain Panel
chunks.text:
- Captain-facing portal showing their stats: losses debited, parcels, general performance
- Captains see loss notifications here, then raise tickets via self-serve portal

chunks.title: Ticket Queues Handled by L1
chunks.text:
1. Losses & Debits
2. Payments
3. Orders & Planning
4. Line Haul
5. Last Mile Pendency
6. Cash Handover (COD)
7. (And others — ~8-10 queues total)

chunks.title: How L1 Agents Work
chunks.text:
- Agents have a shift covering as many tickets as possible
- When stuck → ask trainer on Google Chat (trainer responds same day)
- Night shift: flag uncertain tickets, hold them, answer in morning after trainer review
- For first month: all responses reviewed by trainer before sending (authorization mode)

chunks.title: CRITICAL — "Delivered" Always Means Delivered to END CUSTOMER
chunks.text:
In ALL loss/hardstop scenarios, "delivered" = the FE/driver physically handed the package to the end customer (the person who ordered the item). It does NOT mean the shipment arrived at a hub.

**Scenario 7 specifically**: "Shipment delivered but couldn't be marked" = FE went to the customer's address, gave them the package, but failed to tap "Mark Delivered" in the app. The loss is then wrongly marked on the hub. L1 checks: was it actually delivered to the customer? Resolution: captain should raise issue directly with tech team.

**Never apply Scenario 7** to tickets about missing items at the hub, wrong bags received, or products missing from a box. Those are completely different scenarios (shortage / misroute).

chunks.title: Missing Item from Received Box at Hub
chunks.text:
**Scenario**: A shipment (or bag) arrives at the hub but a product is missing from inside the box. Captain raises a ticket claiming "product missing" or "item not available from box". A loss is being marked.

**What L1 should do**:
1. Check if proof is provided in the ticket (photos, CCTV screenshots, weight discrepancy screenshots, or other attachments)
2. **If proof is shared** → action=escalate, escalation_queue=L2_LOSSES. Remark: "Captain has shared proof of missing item — please review for loss reversal."
3. **If no proof is mentioned or attached** → action=needs_info. Reply: "Dear Captain, To process your reversal request for the missing item, please share photographic/CCTV evidence and the box weight discrepancy report."

**Never use action=stuck for this scenario** — always either needs_info (no proof) or escalate (proof available). The hub cannot self-resolve a loss — it always needs L2 review once proof is shared.

chunks.title: Waiver Eligibility (General Rule)
chunks.text:
A captain qualifies for loss waiver ONLY if ALL conditions are true:
- Tenure < 60 days
- 0 prior waivers/reversals of this loss type
- At least 1 payment cycle invoice already generated
- Current pendency of this loss type = 0
- Not a GST defaulter or COD pendency defaulter (for shortage)

chunks.title: CRITICAL — Seller Dependency / Meesho AM Scenarios
chunks.text:
When a captain says **"no revert from Meesho AM"**, **"highlighted on time but no response from AM"**, **"awaited instruction"**, or any variation of waiting for the seller/Meesho Account Manager — this is **ALWAYS Hardstop Scenario 9 (Seller Dependency)**, NOT Scenarios 8.1/8.2.

- **Meesho AM** = Meesho Account Manager = the seller's point of contact at Meesho
- **Seller Dependency SOP**: For certain shipment situations (customer unreachable, special handling), the captain must await instructions from the seller via the AM. The SOP defines how many attempts to make and within what timeframe.
- If the captain flagged the issue and the AM/seller didn't respond → **Scenario 9.1** (SOP followed by captain, no response from seller = no fault of captain)
- If the captain also breached the SOP on their side → **Scenario 9.2** does NOT apply, stay on 9.1

**How to distinguish from Scenarios 8.1/8.2:**
- 8.1/8.2: Shipment was not inscanned BACK to hub. FE has the shipment or amount is recovered from FE.
- 9.1/9.2: Shipment situation where seller instructions were needed. Captain was waiting, AM didn't respond.
- The signal "Meesho AM", "AM not reverting", "no revert from AM", "highlighted on time" = **Scenario 9**, full stop.

chunks.title: CRITICAL — When There Is No SOP: Always Ask, Never Guess
chunks.text:
Valmo L1 has SOPs for approximately 22 defined scenarios across Shortage, Hardstop, Missing Item, Payments, Consumables, and Orders queues. **If a captain's ticket does not clearly map to one of these scenarios, the correct action is always `needs_info` — ask the captain for clarification.**

Never force-fit a vague ticket into the closest-sounding scenario. Getting the wrong scenario wrong is worse than asking.

chunks.title: Vague Phrases That Always Require Clarification
chunks.text:
| Captain says | What to ask |
|---|---|
| "suspicious shipment" | "Could you clarify what was suspicious — tampered packaging, suspected fraud, wrong contents, incorrect address, or something else?" |
| "issue with shipment" / "some problem" | "Could you describe exactly what the issue is — loss marked, item missing, cannot mark in system, or something else?" |
| "highlighted on time" | "What specifically did you highlight — the shipment status, a customer issue, or a delivery attempt problem?" |
| "wrong loss" (with no AWB or scan context) | Ask for AWB numbers first before any SOP check |
| "please check" / "please resolve" (with no detail) | Ask what specifically needs to be checked or resolved |

chunks.title: What "Suspicious Shipment" Could Mean (Once Captain Clarifies)
chunks.text:
Once the captain explains what "suspicious" means, map it:
- **Tampered/open packaging on arrival at hub** → Missing Item scenario (check proof, escalate L2_LOSSES)
- **Customer suspected of fraud / refuses delivery** → Seller Dependency scenario, check with AM
- **Wrong/unexpected contents in bag** → Shortage or Missing Item depending on context
- **Shipment seems misrouted or wrong AWB** → Hardstop misroute scenario (S10, S11.1, S11.2)
- **Consignment_Lost status on Log10** → Hardstop S8.1 or S8.2

**Do not attempt any of these until the captain confirms what "suspicious" actually means.**

chunks.title: Key Terms
chunks.text:
- **AWB**: Airway Bill — shipment tracking number (Valmo format: VL + 11-13 digits)
- **DC**: Delivery Center
- **Captain**: Delivery partner/executive
- **RTO**: Return to Origin
- **THC**: Truck Handover Confirmation scan
- **FE**: Field Executive (same as captain in some contexts)
- **CN Number**: Credit Note number (for debit-related tickets)
- **Payment Cycle**: Billing period (format: YYYY-WXX)
- **Enbolt ID**: Partner's system ID
- **Attribution**: Assignment of loss responsibility to a specific node/captain

chunks.title: losses_and_debits: hardstop
chunks.text: Shipment arrived at a hub but was not connected forward within SLA (7 days forward / 5 days reverse). System auto-marks loss against the holding hub. Triggers: INWARD_SCAN (or VEHICLE_ARRIVED + MANIFEST_RECEIVED) but no subsequent MANIFEST_SCAN within 7 days (5 for reverse leg). Look for gap between arrival and outward MANIFEST_SCAN.

chunks.title: losses_and_debits: shortage
chunks.text: Item count or condition mismatch at a node — fewer items received than the manifest claimed. SEC_QC_FAIL is the canonical scan trigger. Triggers: SEC_QC_FAIL at a node, or discrepancy between MANIFEST_RECEIVED count vs INWARD_SCAN count

chunks.title: losses_and_debits: misroute
chunks.text: Shipment dispatched to wrong destination hub (sc_migration_issue). Captain has 2 days from VEHICLE_ARRIVED at wrong hub to re-MANIFEST_SCAN. Beyond 2 days = hardstop on original hub. Triggers: VEHICLE_ARRIVED at hub != intended destination, then MISROUTE-tagged scan or no re-routing within 2 days

chunks.title: losses_and_debits: pilot_lost_on_field
chunks.text: FE left hub with shipment but neither delivered nor returned. CONSIGNMENT_MARKED_LOST follows OFD_SCAN with no DELIVERED/UNDELIVERED. Triggers: OFD_SCAN  →  CONSIGNMENT_MARKED_LOST (with no DELIVERED or UNDELIVERED in between)

chunks.title: losses_and_debits: not_inscanned
chunks.text: No INWARD_SCAN at captain's hub — captain claims shipment never reached them. Triggers: Absence of INWARD_SCAN at the disputed hub. Check LM FE Loss Marked for amount-recovered status.

chunks.title: losses_and_debits: delivered_unrecorded
chunks.text: FE physically handed parcel to customer but didn't mark DELIVERED in app — system marks loss because no DELIVERED scan exists. Triggers: OFD_SCAN exists, no DELIVERED, no UNDELIVERED, no CONSIGNMENT_MARKED_LOST. Captain provides POD/photo proof.

chunks.title: losses_and_debits: tech_no_loss
chunks.text: Pure tech/scan failure without loss — captain reports something not working but no loss marked yet. Triggers: N/A — no scan check needed. Redirect to Log10 team.

chunks.title: losses_and_debits: tech_with_loss
chunks.text: Tech failure that caused or compounded a loss event (e.g. couldn't initiate RTO scan due to app crash, loss got marked). Triggers: Mixed signal: tech-failure language + actual loss marking. Captain has proof (screenshot/error).
tags: scan not working AND loss marked

chunks.title: QC failure
chunks.text:
Scenario: QC failure | L1 SOP: Step 1: Identify the loss marked AWB* When the Captain raises a request stating that the FE has been marked with a wrong loss, the agent needs to first enter the **FE ID or Phone Number** in query:https://metabase-main.bi.meeshogcp.in/question/187207-qc-fail* Apply the **exact time range** when the loss was marked.* The Captain will share the **loss marked amount**.* The agent needs to locate the same amount on the mentioned date and identify the **AWB number** against which the loss was marked.
Step 2: Check the debit reason* Enter the identified **AWB number** in query:https://metabase-main.bi.meeshogcp.in/question/187207-qc-fail* Check the **debit_reason** column.* The possible debit reasons are:* LM Secondary QC junk image* Pickup junk image* ICUD junk image* Pickup vs QC image mismatch.
Step 3: Identify the node where loss was marked* If the debit reason is **LM Secondary QC junk image** â Loss is marked on the **LM Hub**.* If the debit reason is **Pickup junk image** â Loss is marked on the **LM FE (Pickup FE)**.* If the debit reason is **ICUD junk image** â Loss is marked on the **LM FE (ICUD FE - Forward leg)**.* If the debit reason is **Pickup vs QC image mismatch** â Loss is marked on the **LM FE**.
Step 4: Validate images and take action* The agent needs to review the images captured at the respective node where the loss was marked.* Check whether the entity has followed the required SOP.* If the entity has followed the SOP â The marked loss will be reversed.* If the entity has not followed the SOP â The loss will be considered valid and the entity will bear the loss. | Escalation: Direct L2 escaltion if a image mismatch is ientified by L1 agent | L2 SOP: 1. L2 agent must review the ticket and the loss marked.
2. If the loss is correctly marked then response to captain
3. If the loss is rightly contested then L2 agent must raise it for reversal on the dashboard made by the SX team.
4. SX team is to check the loss and confirm the re-attribution within 48 hours. | Functional dependency: L3 - SX claims | TAT:  | POCs:  | Template:

The Status could be: Blacklisted Temporarily Inactive 2. If the status shows Blacklisted , Escalate to L2. 3. If the status shows temporarily Inactive, check in the Autopay Whitelist L2 query, This query will show two reasons broadly: Not - Whitelisted Whitelisted 4. If the query shows Not - Whitelisted, ask the FE to be onboarded on Valmo1. In case of Whitelisted, check the\ LM Pilot Payout Query\ https://metabase-main.bi.meeshogcp.in/question/181646-lm-pilot-payout-raw to check if any COD debit has been marked. 5. If the COD debit of \> 1500 has been marked, send this\ form\ https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSd5u0HcS7JBBt2F1B6UihNsjIIMN1OReoXFJC4wSlUmRkxelg/viewform to the FE to be filled by him. | | |

chunks.title: Payments: UTR generated & payment processed but not reflected in FE's account
chunks.text: Scenario (Payments 4.3.10): FE says 'UTR generated, payment processed, but I did not get it'. Required inputs from the partner: Partner ID, FE phone number, payment Cycle, Transaction ID. L1 process: check the Razorpay Reversal Sheet for the Transaction ID. If the transaction ID IS present (reversed) -> explain the reversal to the FE and the expected reprocess date. If the transaction ID is ABSENT -> Razorpay holds no reversed amount, so this is a BANK-SIDE issue: clearly tell the FE that and exactly what to ask their bank (a UTR trace). Do not promise a Valmo-side credit when the money already left our system. TAT 72h.

chunks.title: Cash Handover: COD shortfall mistakenly marked on the Pilot
chunks.text: Scenario (Cash Handover): FE/Pilot says 'a COD shortfall was marked on me by mistake'. Required inputs: FE Partner ID, FE phone number, date of shortfall. L1 process: check the LM Pilot Payout Raw query for the debit. If the FE was debited ABOVE Rs.1,500 -> share the COD-shortfall correction form to be filled by the DC Captain -> then TRACK the reversal through and confirm the amount is credited back to the FE before closing (do NOT close at form submission). Also ensure the FE IDs get REACTIVATED after the COD adjustment.

chunks.title: Losses: Wrong loss marked — shipment damaged / scan issues (Hardstop S12)
chunks.text: Scenario (Hardstop S12): Captain claims a wrong loss was marked because the shipment was DAMAGED or had SCAN ISSUES. This is NOT auto-reversed by L1. L1 process: inform the partner they must fill the damaged-shipment form to mark the shipment as damaged. The ticket is then escalated directly to L3, who reviews the form + the evidence and reverses any loss marked. Damaged-shipment form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSes4awhzpR7mTe8Ti5ZrlG7lzrwrMyJlF13XCn2zaIwH-8c7Q/viewform

chunks.title: Others: FE ID deactivated → reactivation (decision tree)
chunks.text: Scenario: a captain/FE says their FE ID is deactivated and wants it reactivated. Required inputs: FE ID, FE phone number. This is a DECISION TREE — walk it, do not jump to a form. Step 1: check the FE's status in Log10 — it is either 'Blacklisted' or 'Temporarily Inactive'. Step 2: if BLACKLISTED -> escalate to L2 (no form). Step 3: if TEMPORARILY INACTIVE -> check the Autopay Whitelist L2 query, which shows 'Not Whitelisted' or 'Whitelisted'. Step 4: if NOT WHITELISTED -> ask the FE to be onboarded on Valmo1. If WHITELISTED -> check the LM Pilot Payout query for any COD debit marked. Step 5: ONLY IF a COD debit greater than Rs.1500 has been marked -> send the FE this correction form to fill: https://forms.gle/BiPJ5uRYRaQLXrAu8. IMPORTANT: that form applies ONLY to the COD-debit-over-1500 case; never send it for the blacklisted or not-whitelisted branches.
